$comment: Recipe consumed by ~/.claude/skills/bump/SKILL.md (and /release). Rolodex carries its version in CHANGELOG.md + the git tag only (no __version__ in the single-file app), so the 'source of truth' is the topmost dated CHANGELOG heading. The bump cuts the accumulated [Unreleased] block into a dated [{NEW}] section and advances the compare-link footer. post_check asserts the new section + footer links are present and agree. Tag v{NEW} is what triggers .github/workflows/build.yml to build + attach the self-contained Linux/Windows/macOS binaries.

files.$comment: Cut [Unreleased] into a dated release section, leaving a fresh empty [Unreleased] above it.
Advance the Unreleased compare range to the new tag and add the new release's tag link.

post_check: V=$(sed -n 's/^## \[\([0-9.]*\)\] - .*/\1/p' CHANGELOG.md | head -1); test -n "$V" && grep -q "^## \[$V\] - " CHANGELOG.md && grep -qF "compare/v$V...HEAD" CHANGELOG.md && grep -qE "^\[$V\]: .*releases/tag/v$V" CHANGELOG.md && echo "version lockstep OK: $V" || { echo "VERSION DRIFT: expected a dated ## [$V] section plus matching compare + tag footer links in CHANGELOG.md"; exit 1; }
todos: Confirm the new ## [{NEW}] CHANGELOG section reads correctly — its bullets were authored during development via changelog_log, so the bump only cuts the [Unreleased] block into a dated section (no body to write from scratch).

Publish: after the release commit lands on origin/main, run `git tag v{NEW} && git push origin v{NEW}`. That triggers .github/workflows/build.yml, which builds the self-contained single-file Linux/Windows/macOS binaries on their native runners and attaches them to the GitHub Release. (A normal push to main does NOT trigger a build.)

Optional pre-publish validation (free on a public repo): `gh workflow run "Build binaries"` builds + self-tests all three OSes WITHOUT publishing (the release-attach step only fires on a v* tag). Confirm all three are green before tagging.
